description_long: This object consists of two visually similar shotguns, each featuring a wooden full stock and a metallic barrel assembly. The barrels are long and cylindrical, with prominent vent holes along the upper portion, and both have drum magazines attached beneath the receiver. The metallic components are finished in different colors: one in a dark black and the other in a brass-like tone, while the stocks are a reddish-brown wood with visible grain and some wear. The trigger guard is rounded and encloses a standard trigger, and the fore-end is integrated with the barrel assembly. The overall design is robust, with utilitarian features suitable for urban or military use. The shotguns appear to be semi-automatic with no visible pump or lever mechanisms.
description: A pair of robust, wood-stocked shotguns with long vented barrels and drum magazines, finished in black and brass metal. The design is utilitarian, with rounded trigger guards and visible wear on the wooden stocks.
description_view_0: Side view showing both shotguns' full wooden stocks, long vented barrels, and drum magazines; clear contrast between black and brass finishes.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the circular drum magazines and the alignment of the barrels; the vent holes and metallic finishes are visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full stocks, vented barrels, and drum magazines, with the color difference between the two shotguns.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the underside of the drum magazines and the lower barrel assembly; the stocks and trigger guards are less visible.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel with vented upper rib and long cylindrical profile
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with smooth edges and no visible finger grooves
